cases.title: Keep Optique source patches sparse
cases.prompt: A command has --output with a parser default of ./dist, APP_OUTPUT in the environment, output in c12 config, and a resolved Zod default. Explain the bug and provide a corrected Optique/source-resolution pattern that preserves the intended precedence.
rubric: Removes parser default from a source-bearing term
Applies the default only after source precedence
Shows a concrete parser and resolver boundary
cases.rationale: Parser defaults are a recurring source-precedence failure.

cases.title: Respect mixed Optique version boundaries
cases.prompt: Review a Deno CLI whose lockfile contains stable @optique/core and @optique/run 1.1.1 plus several 1.2.0-dev integration packages. A proposed patch copies current docs and widens all versions to caret ranges. Diagnose the risk and define the verification required before changing versions.
rubric: Does not assume documentation matches installed exports
Keeps compatible Optique packages on a proven line
Requires packaged-artifact verification
cases.rationale: The attached implementation contains a real stable/prerelease split.

cases.title: Keep commands visible in a standalone binary
cases.prompt: Design Optique command registration for a CLI that will be bundled and compiled into a standalone executable while preserving completion and man generation. Include the failure oracle that proves every command made it into the artifact.
rubric: Uses one statically visible command model
Generates help/completion/man from that model
Executes each installed command
cases.rationale: Source discovery can succeed while compiled commands disappear.

cases.title: Keep prompt integrations automation safe
cases.prompt: Use @optique/clack to ask for every missing configuration value. The command will run in CI and with redirected stdin. Define a safe interaction policy and subprocess tests without removing the interactive experience.
rubric: Treats Clack as a renderer adapter
Provides non-interactive representations
Prevents hangs in CI
cases.rationale: A prompt package does not own the automation policy.

cases.title: Compose array operations in precedence order
cases.prompt: Resolve routes when file provides [file], environment prepends [env], and CLI appends [cli]. The public merge call lists inputs highest-to-lowest. Give the correct result, traversal order, and defu boundary needed to avoid operation objects merging into each other.
rubric: Produces [env, file, cli]
Distinguishes caller precedence from evaluation order
Uses pairwise resolved inheritance
cases.rationale: Operation composition is the most fragile part of the merge contract.

cases.title: Replace config union branches atomically
cases.prompt: A lower config selects {kind: range, from, to, limit}; a higher config selects {kind: named, crawls}. defu retains from/to/limit. Explain and correct the merger without replacing every nested object atomically.
rubric: Rejects hybrid branches
Targets the schema-known path
Preserves recursive merging for ordinary objects
cases.rationale: Generic recursive merging corrupts discriminated unions.

cases.title: Prove one dynamic config evaluation
cases.prompt: A two-pass Optique CLI uses c12 to load an async TypeScript config factory, and handlers need the resolved configuration. Design the control flow and a real fixture oracle proving the factory evaluates once without hiding calls behind a reset.
rubric: Loads at the composition/source-context boundary
Shares the resolved value
Uses an executable side-effect counter
cases.rationale: Static inspection cannot prove dynamic factories execute once.

cases.title: Implement a real config explain contract
cases.prompt: Design config files and config explain machine results for a layered c12 configuration with extends, environment, CLI override, and array operations. Include redaction and explain why a static precedence list is insufficient.
rubric: Uses recorded resolver decisions
Reports every contributing layer
Returns versioned redacted machine data
cases.rationale: Configuration inspection must answer field-specific decisions.

cases.title: Isolate raw stdout from diagnostic sinks
cases.prompt: Configure LogTape so app result JSON, completion scripts, and man pages pass through LogTape but reach stdout as exact raw bytes while app diagnostics reach stderr or a file. Include the category rule that prevents inherited sinks.
rubric: Uses separate hierarchical categories
Gives result a raw sink
Blocks parent diagnostic inheritance
cases.rationale: One transport requires category isolation to preserve composability.

cases.title: Redact all LogTape routes before formatting
cases.prompt: A config show command JSON.stringify()s its full config into result_text and then applies LogTape field redaction to the sink. It also writes pretty diagnostics and an error file. Correct the design and define tests for nested secrets across every route.
rubric: Keeps sensitive objects structured until redaction
Wraps result and every diagnostic sink
Tests bootstrap and nested values
cases.rationale: Field redaction cannot inspect opaque serialized values.

cases.title: Honor logging options when configuration fails
cases.prompt: A CLI config file fails during c12 evaluation before the full Optique program runs. The user passed --log-format json --log-output errors.jsonl --silent. Design the smallest bootstrap path and exact process assertions without duplicating the domain parser.
rubric: Parses only early logging controls
Configures a redacted bootstrap graph
Asserts exact stdout/file/exit behavior
cases.rationale: Fallible configuration precedes normal logger setup.

cases.title: Keep durable stage data out of logger persistence
cases.prompt: A proposal replaces a typed JSONL StageWriter and checkpoint store with LogTape file sinks because LogTape is the sole output transport. Correct the ownership model and show how stage writes can still emit diagnostics.
rubric: Separates observable transport from durable application state
Keeps typed artifact validation
Allows structured diagnostic events
cases.rationale: Sole process output transport does not make a logger the data store.

cases.title: Select focused UnJS adapters by ownership
cases.prompt: Design a project-aware CLI that loads layered config, detects CI/color, edits TypeScript config, performs bounded HTTP requests, persists resumable checkpoints, detects the project package manager, and builds a distributable library. Select focused UnJS packages, their boundaries, and what each does not own.
rubric: Maps each capability to one focused adapter
Keeps domain policy above packages
Does not install unrelated ecosystem packages
cases.rationale: The ecosystem is valuable only when package ownership remains explicit.

cases.title: Reject overlapping parser and logger stacks
cases.prompt: Add Citty beneath Optique for nested commands and Consola beside LogTape for progress because all four packages are popular. Evaluate the proposal and provide the narrower implementation choices.
rubric: Chooses one parser owner
Chooses one output transport
Implements progress over the selected event model
cases.rationale: Ecosystem accumulation creates conflicting public contracts.

cases.title: Do not confuse unstorage with durability
cases.prompt: A local CLI writes progress to an unstorage memory driver and hashes arguments with ohash. Announce that the command is now durable and exactly-once across crashes. Correct the claim and define the missing recovery contracts and kill/restart tests.
rubric: Rejects the durability claim
Separates storage/hash utilities from workflow semantics
Requires executable recovery tests
cases.rationale: Persistence adapters do not establish exactly-once recovery.

cases.title: Trace one public CLI value through every owner
cases.prompt: Review a --timeout option that appears in Optique help, APP_TIMEOUT, c12 config, Zod runtime schema, ofetch policy, generated completion, and docs. Produce an end-to-end ownership trace, identify ambiguous defaults, and define executable verification without editing.
rubric: Separates grammar, sources, runtime validation, and consumer
Names one default owner
Includes generated and installed surfaces
cases.rationale: A public term is incomplete when any ownership arrow is missing.

cases.title: Keep focused UnJS packages behind application contracts
cases.prompt: Sketch the adapter boundaries for a CLI using ofetch, unstorage, ohash, and nypm. For each package, name the application policy it must not decide and one executable test that crosses the real adapter.
rubric: Assigns focused adapter roles
Keeps domain policy above the packages
Uses real integration or process oracles
cases.rationale: A seen case teaches selection without reducing the ecosystem to package-name recall.

cases.title: Compose a complete normal CLI invocation
cases.prompt: Describe the complete control flow for a successful command from raw argv through early controls, c12 loading, Optique sources, resolved Zod request, injected handler capabilities, LogTape result routing, cleanup, and installed-artifact verification. Identify which stages execute once.
rubric: Preserves source and lifecycle ownership
Avoids handler-global reads and duplicate loads
Ends with the exact user artifact
cases.rationale: A seen composition case prevents individually correct references from producing a broken lifecycle.

cases.title: Audit Optique c12 defu LogTape and Zod ownership together
cases.prompt: Review a CLI that uses Optique, c12, defu, LogTape, and Zod. Determine whether the libraries are being used together ideally. Produce a boundary-by-boundary audit and identify concrete fixes for defaults, config loading, merging, logging, and verification.
rubric: Assigns one owner per boundary
Rejects parser defaults as sparse source values
Requires one resolved config and logger resource for handlers
Includes executable verification, not only static inspection
cases.rationale: The hardest failures happen at ownership boundaries between otherwise good libraries.

cases.title: Recall the five-library stack ownership map
cases.prompt: A CLI uses Optique, c12, defu, LogTape, and Zod. Give the correct owner for token grammar, config discovery, loader merging, app merge semantics, runtime defaults, diagnostics/results, and handler execution. Include one failure signature for each boundary.
rubric: Maps every library to its boundary
Names what each library must not own
Includes concrete failure signatures
cases.rationale: Agents need the integrated stack map before applying detailed implementation rules.

cases.title: Apply the stack map to one timeout option
cases.prompt: Trace a --timeout value through Optique help and parsing, APP_TIMEOUT, c12 config, defu-backed app merging, Zod defaults, an HTTP adapter, provenance, LogTape diagnostics, and subprocess tests. Show where the common parser-default bug would enter.
rubric: Traces one public value through all owners
Identifies the parser-default pollution point
Ends in executable verification
cases.rationale: A seen value trace proves the reference is usable, not just conceptually complete.

cases.title: Teach the full five-library CLI mental model
cases.prompt: Give another agent a detailed implementation mental model for a Deno CLI using Optique, c12, defu, LogTape, and Zod. Include diagrams or maps, wrong/right examples, ownership tables, defaulting logic, the actual merge algorithm, and a verification matrix detailed enough to prevent common boundary mistakes.
rubric: Explains data flow through every library boundary
Shows concrete wrong and right implementation shapes
Details the c12 loader merger, app merger, operation lowering, and final sparse validation
Distinguishes documented defaults, parser defaults, deferred fallbacks, and Zod runtime defaults
Defines tests that catch each major ownership regression
cases.rationale: The skill must transfer enough structure that another agent can implement safely, not merely recall package names.

cases.title: Use Optique 1.2 deferred values without stealing Zod defaults
cases.prompt: A CLI sees Optique 1.2 deferredValue and proposes moving every config default from Zod into deferred parser fallbacks so help can show the values. Correct the design, including documented defaults, handler-time fallbacks, placeholders, and tests.
rubric: Limits deferredValue to real handler-time fallbacks
Keeps ordinary runtime defaults in the complete schema
Prevents placeholders from entering patches or provenance
Tests help-visible defaults separately from parsed output
cases.rationale: Optique 1.2 makes deferred values tempting, but most defaults still belong to schemas.

cases.title: Separate c12 loader merging from strict app merging
cases.prompt: A c12 CLI passes its final strict mergeConfigInputs function as loadConfig({ merger }) and then config extends plus $append fails. Explain why this is wrong and define the two merger functions, the high-to-low versus low-to-high app merge loop, standalone operation lowering, and fixtures that prove the fix.
rubric: Keeps c12 control metadata alive during loading
Runs strict app merge after c12 resolves layers
Explains standalone operation lowering and reversed evaluation
Proves extends plus array operation composition through real config files and unit merge cases
cases.rationale: The single-merger shortcut breaks loader semantics before application validation can help.

cases.title: Separate CLI startup from component throughput
cases.prompt: Define the minimum benchmark model for a Deno CLI using Optique, c12, defu, Zod, provenance, and LogTape. Distinguish installed startup, cold application work, warm component throughput, fixtures, correctness gates, and the statistics required for a regression claim.
rubric: Defines process startup separately from reusable in-process work
Names representative deterministic workloads for each library boundary
Requires semantic output checks before timing
Reports a distribution, sample context, and meaningful absolute and relative changes
cases.rationale: Agents need a baseline measurement vocabulary before reviewing a specific performance claim.

cases.title: Keep Optique Zod adapters sparse and choose the right Zod default
cases.prompt: Implement an --output option shared with APP_OUTPUT and c12 config. The value trims whitespace and removes a trailing slash, help should show ./dist, and omission from every authored source should resolve to ./dist. Explain the exact @optique/zod term, sparse and complete schemas, whether to use Zod v4 .default() or .prefault(), and tests that prove the placeholder and help default never become CLI provenance.
rubric: Uses @optique/zod only for scalar token parsing and keeps omission absent
Uses a non-defaulted sparse leaf and a complete runtime schema
Chooses prefault because the fallback must pass through normalization
Separately tests help visibility, parsed absence, precedence, and provenance
cases.rationale: The adapter boundary is where parser-visible metadata is most often mistaken for runtime default ownership.

cases.title: Preserve explicit source identity when values are equal
cases.prompt: Config sets retries=3 and the user explicitly passes --retries=3. A proposed config explain implementation compares values and reports config as the winner because nothing changed. Correct the algorithm and define a versioned, redacted provenance envelope that also handles aliases, --name=value, runtime defaults, arrays, and shadowed contributions.
rubric: Rejects value comparison as the provenance oracle
Records explicit presence and source metadata during resolution
Represents winners, shadowed values, operations, source IDs, and runtime defaults
States evidence limits when Optique does not expose source identity directly
cases.rationale: Equal values are the simplest counterexample to provenance reconstructed after merging.

cases.title: Benchmark the five-library stack without weakening semantics
cases.prompt: A change claims the Optique/c12/defu/Zod/LogTape startup path is 40% faster based on one warm in-process merge loop. Review the claim and design a reproducible Deno/mitata benchmark plan covering installed startup, config discovery, merge/provenance scaling, Zod parsing, logging/redaction, fixtures, statistics, and regression gates.
rubric: Rejects the warm microbenchmark as evidence for startup
Separates cold process, warm component, and long-running measurements
Uses deterministic representative fixtures and validates outputs before timing
Reports absolute and relative changes with variability and a calibrated budget
cases.rationale: Component throughput and user-visible command latency require different evidence.

cases.title: Prove config defaults and merger behavior through real boundaries
cases.prompt: Create a verification plan for a Deno CLI refactor that separates c12's loader merger from the strict app merger, moves runtime defaults into Zod, adds field provenance, and threads one config snapshot through Optique hooks. Include unit, temporary-project, subprocess, generated-surface, installed-artifact, and benchmark gates with concrete failure oracles.
rubric: Separates merger unit oracles from real c12 loader fixtures
Proves one factory evaluation with an external side effect
Tests default absence, equal-valued overrides, array operations, atomic unions, and redaction
Exercises exact streams and exits through the installed artifact before benchmarking
cases.rationale: The refactor crosses enough boundaries that no single test layer can establish correctness.

cases.title: Trace browser detect from argv to WACZ artifacts
cases.prompt: Using the Kaiju CLI casebook, trace `kaiju browser detect --route example.com --capture-profile max --wacz --out out/browser` from argv through Optique parsing, sparse adapter patching, config/provenance overlay, runtime Zod defaults, browser run orchestration, stages, WARC/WACZ artifacts, and result output. Identify at least five failure oracles.
rubric: Follows the complete public-token to artifact/result trace
Distinguishes profile-implied source policy from ordinary runtime defaults
Preserves sparse adapter and provenance rules
Names concrete stage, archive, resume/overwrite, and WACZ failure oracles
cases.rationale: The browser command combines nearly every CLI boundary and catches generic advice.

cases.title: Keep stages artifacts results and diagnostics separate
cases.prompt: A patch proposes writing raw captured bytes into LogTape diagnostic records and stage JSONL so browser and Common Crawl runs have fewer artifact files. Use the Kaiju casebook to reject or revise the design. Include the stage envelope, binary payload rule, result/diagnostic split, and verification matrix.
rubric: Separates stable results, operational diagnostics, and durable artifacts
Rejects raw binary in stage payloads and LogTape diagnostics
Documents the stage JSONL envelope and typed artifact reference pattern
Requires tests for stdout/stderr isolation, redaction, stage validation, and artifact readback
cases.rationale: Artifact/log boundary mistakes are high-risk in capture pipelines.

cases.title: Separate ideal cancellation from observed implementation
cases.prompt: Review a Kaiju-style long-running CLI and decide whether cancellation is actually productionized. Trace AbortController ownership, browser queue behavior, ordered async mapping, worker pool close, stage/diagnostic flush, archive close, and overwrite markers. State what is implemented, what is only an ideal target, and what tests are required.
rubric: Does not claim cancellation is complete from types alone
Traces signal ownership from process entry to active work
Covers queue, worker, stage, diagnostics, archive, and overwrite lifecycle
Ends with concrete subprocess and cleanup tests
cases.rationale: Cancellation and cleanup are easy to overclaim without end-to-end signal tracing.
